prompt:
We're standing up a tiny internal inventory API and I want it to be a proper little Flask app, not a script. The repo already has requirements.txt with our pinned versions and the run_verify.sh that our CI box runs — but nothing is installed on this machine yet (there's no virtualenv here at all). Set the environment up the way we standardize on for small services — a local .venv in the project with the pinned requirements installed into it — and then build app.py:

- a create_app() application factory (the tests build fresh instances; don't leave a module-level app lying around)
- an 'api' blueprint mounted under /api with: GET /api/items returning {"items": [...]} ordered by id; POST /api/items taking JSON {"name": "..."} and returning 201 with {"id": n, "name": "..."} (ids start at 1 and count up per app instance); a request that isn't JSON, or is missing a name, or has a blank name gets 400 with {"error": "name is required"}; GET /api/items/<id> returning the item or a 404
- JSON errors everywhere: unknown routes and wrong methods must come back as JSON like {"error": "not found"} / {"error": "method not allowed"} — never an HTML error page (we have a dashboard that parses these)
- each create_app() instance keeps its own in-memory store — two instances must not see each other's items (that bit us in tests before)

When you're done, `bash run_verify.sh` must pass — it runs pytest out of the project venv.
